steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage gives two distinct word counts: Costa Rica's constitution at 16,705 words and Portugal's at 35,219 words. The sentence uses "these [blank]" as the subject of "correlate," so the blank must be a plural noun that captures both lengths, and it must show possession (the lengths belong to the constitutions).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘constitutions' length,’: uses a singular noun despite there being two distinct word counts. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘constitution's length,’: still singular, which doesn't match two separate measurements. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘constitutions' lengths,’: plural possessive correctly shows the lengths belong to the two constitutions, and the plural noun 'lengths' matches the two distinct values. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘constitutions lengths,’: uses plural 'lengths' but omits the apostrophe, so it doesn't show that the lengths belong to the constitutions. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
